item.title: 7/10: The Takeout with Major Garrett
item.description: President Trump says U.S. continuing Iran talks, but ceasefire is "over"; Graham Platner officially withdraws from Maine Senate race.

item.title: Pentagon taking steps to address troops who fail to adhere to grooming and fitness standards
item.description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth is renewing his push to tighten grooming and fitness standards for American service members. CBS News' Lindsey Reiser has more.

item.title: Pentagon releases new set of UFO files: "Unlike anything I had seen"
item.description: The U.S. military released a new batch of files related to UFOs, including one report from a Navy pilot who said a mysterious object was "unlike anything I had seen" in 28 years of service.

item.title: Iran admits to Trump advisers "they made a mistake" in shooting at ships in strait
item.description: The White House wants Iran to publicly acknowledge that shooting at the ships was a mistake.

item.title: This week on "Sunday Morning" (July 12)
item.description: A look at the features for this week's broadcast of the Emmy-winning program, hosted by Jane Pauley.

item.title: Cyclosporiasis parasite cases found in 31 states
item.description: Cases of cyclosporiasis, a disease caused by a microscopic parasite, are growing in 31 states, and health officials still haven't identified the exact source of the outbreak. Dr. Eric Ascher, a family medicine physician, joins "The Daily Report" to discuss the illness.

item.title: Trump admin. to back tariffs on Russian oil in effort to end Ukraine war, sources say
item.description: The war in Ukraine is now in its fifth year. A bipartisan bill would impose heavy financial penalties on purchasers of Russian oil,

item.title: ICE officer killed Mexican man while looking for different person in Houston, DHS says
item.description: The Department of Homeland Security said Immigration and Customs Enforcement officers were looking for a different person when a Mexican man was shot and killed in Houston on Tuesday. CBS News' Shawna Mizelle reports.

item.title: Iran told Trump advisers it "made a mistake" in shooting at ships
item.description: Iran told the Trump administration privately that it "made a mistake" in shooting at commercial ships in the Strait of Hormuz, senior U.S. officials said. The attack on three ships led to two days of intense strikes. CBS News political reporter Zak Hudak has more.

item.title: Curtis Sliwa says "it's time for Bibi to go" after poll finds Jewish Americans favor Mamdani more
item.description: A new poll from The Associated Press and the National Opinion Research Center finds that New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani has a higher favorability rating among Jewish Americans than Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu. Curtis Sliwa, who faced off against Mamdani as the Republican mayoral candidate, joins with his reaction.

item.title: What we know about the Mississippi teen's drowning death and investigation
item.description: The family of 18-year-old Nolan Wells and their attorney Ben Crump called for an independent investigation into his death. Wells was reportedly missing on July Fourth after spending the day with his friends on Horn Island off the coast of Mississippi. CBS News correspondent Skyler Henry has more.

item.title: Spain beats Belgium to secure spot in FIFA World Cup semifinals
item.description: Spain beat Belgium to make the 2026 FIFA World Cup semifinals and will play France on Tuesday. Meanwhile, England and Norway are set to face off on Saturday. MLS commentator Calen Carr joins "The Takeout" to share his analysis.

item.title: Parents of teen found dead on island don't believe he chose to stay behind
item.description: Christine and Elmore Wonsley said they don't believe their son would've stayed on the island when his friends left by boat.

item.title: Key takeaways from accused Charlie Kirk killer's weeklong pre-trial hearing
item.description: The weeklong pre-trial hearing for the man accused of killing conservative activist Charlie Kirk wrapped up on Friday with the defense calling one final witness to the stand. CBS News legal contributor Jessica Levinson joins with analysis.

item.title: Apple sues OpenAI, accusing ChatGPT maker of stealing trade secrets
item.description: Apple alleges that OpenAI and two of its employees stole trade secrets and engaged in a "pattern of misconduct."

item.title: U.S. and Iran pause fighting after 2 days of intense strikes
item.description: President Trump again said on Friday that the ceasefire with Iran is "over," but that the U.S. will continue negotiating. CBS News' Margaret Brennan and Elliot Ackerman have the latest.

item.title: Maine Senate candidate on push to unseat Susan Collins, Platner replacement process
item.description: Graham Platner is officially out of the Maine Senate race after facing sexual assault allegations. Democratic candidate Dan Kleban joins CBS News to make his case as Platner's replacement.

item.title: HHS compiling list of injuries eligible for automatic COVID vaccine compensation
item.description: The Health and Human Services department is compiling a list of injuries that would make COVID vaccine recipients automatically eligible for compensation. Dr. Céline Gounder joins The Takeout to explain what this means.

item.title: Graham Platner formally drops out of Maine Senate race
item.description: Graham Platner had until 5 p.m. on Monday to formally suspend his campaign or he would remain on the ballot in November.

item.title: Landmark bipartisan housing bill to become law without Trump's signature
item.description: The landmark bipartisan housing bill is set to become law overnight into Saturday despite President Trump's refusal to sign it. CBS News chief White House correspondent Nancy Cordes reports.

item.title: Watch: Pentagon releases 19 new UFO videos
item.description: The Defense Department released a fourth batch of UFO files on Friday, nearly one month after its third drop. These are all of the videos in the latest tranche, plus analysis from astrophysicist Avi Loeb.

item.title: ICE agents weren't wearing body cameras when Mexican man was fatally shot, agency says
item.description: ICE said immigration agents weren't wearing body cameras when they shot and killed a Mexican man in Houston. Texas lawmakers are demanding a halt to the use of unmarked cars in ICE operations. CBS News Homeland Security correspondent Nicole Sganga reports.

item.title: Astrophysicist Avi Loeb on Pentagon's 4th UFO files release
item.description: The Pentagon released more UFO files on Friday, its fourth tranche since May. Avi Loeb, Baird professor of science at Harvard University and leader of the White House UAP council, joins with analysis.

item.title: Nolan Wells' family demands answers after he was found dead following a boat trip with friends
item.description: A Mississippi family is demanding answers after the death of their 18-year-old son. Nolan Wells took a boat trip with friends to an island off the coast on the Fourth of July, but he never made it back home. His body was found a few days later.

item.title: Trump administration to support heavy tariffs on Russian oil in bid to end Ukraine war, sources say
item.description: In the effort to end the Russia-Ukraine war, a bipartisan group of senators says the Trump administration is on board with legislation to impose more sanctions on Russia. CBS News congressional reporter Taurean Small has the details.

item.title: The 2026 FIFA World Cup schedule and how to watch
item.description: With 104 World Cup games being played in the U.S., Canada and Mexico, it's like "a Super Bowl every single day for five weeks," U.S. team captain Tim Ream told CBS News.

item.title: Trump again says Iran ceasefire is over, but U.S. will keep talking
item.description: Efforts are underway to get U.S.-Iran peace talks back on track after the most intense exchange of attacks since the ceasefire took effect.

item.title: Man partially sucked out of plane window during flight, passenger says
item.description: A woman says she saw a fellow passenger on her Ryanair flight get his head and shoulders sucked out of a window that broke during their trip between Greece and Germany.

item.title: Prince Harry, Meghan visit King Charles after security controversy
item.description: Prince Harry and Meghan have a rocky relationship with the prince's father.

item.title: Trump not expected to veto housing bill, U.S. official says
item.description: President Trump said Friday he won't sign the law, but a U.S. official said he isn't expected to veto it either.
